rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE NOBORU AND AYAKO MURAKAMI REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Requesting Injunctive Relief
rulings.Rulings: Tentative ruling by Judge Ross C. Moody. Petitioner Sandi Fuerte's Motion Requesting Injunctive Relief is DENIED. Petitioner seeks injunctive relief barring and enjoining Respondent and Trustee, Diane Akers, from (1) acting as Trustee; (2) acting as or in a fiduciary capacity for the Trust, the Petitioner, and all other Trust Beneficiaries; and (3) physically and electronically touching, clicking, moving, disposing, dispensing, withdrawing, distributing, or otherwise intentionally and/or recklessly spending the money associated with the net proceeds from the real property sale pertaining to 518 22nd Avenue, pending the outcome of the litigation. Petitioner's stated grounds for the injunction are two-fold: (i) The imminent likelihood that the net proceeds from the sale of the real property will be moved or spent before the litigation can be resolved; and (ii) Respondent's failure to fulfill her legal duty, as fiduciary and Trustee, to provide an annual trust accounting. Here, the Court finds that there has been an insufficient showing that exigent circumstances exist justifying the relief sought. At the time the instant motion was filed in July, Petitioner raised questions regarding the disbursement of the sale proceeds and the 2022 trust accounting that Respondent concedes was overdue. However, since that time, Respondent has provided information relevant to both issues. When the new information is considered, the claims of urgent and exigent circumstances become unpersuasive. First, Respondent has provided Petitioner (and filed with the Court) an account statement showing the sale proceeds from the property sale are on deposit in an account currently being held in trust by Respondent, as trustee. Based on the evidence provided, the proceeds have been on deposit since April 2023, which aligns with the close of escrow on the subject property. Accordingly, there are no facts before the Court supporting the allegation (made on information and belief) that the proceeds have been misdirected or will be moved or improperly spent before the resolution of this litigation, let alone any showing of imminent likelihood of harm. Second, the overdue accounting has been completed. Specifically, as of August 11, 2023, Respondent filed a petition in this court for the approval of her trust accounting for 2022 and the matter is currently set for hearing on October 11, 2023. To the extent the accounting contains material defects, the Court will address any objections to the accounting. Likewise, the Petition to Compel Redress of Breach of Trust is set for hearing on the same day, and Petitioner's claims of trustee malfeasance can and will be adjudicated consistent with the requirements of the Probate Code. The Court, however, does not find a likelihood of irreparable harm or injury on this record that warrants Respondent's immediate removal or suspension pending the outcome of the litigation. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE AKIKO SPINDLE 2021 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/6/24 to be heard with next accounting in conservatorship. (Hearing will likely be continued again after the conservatorship accounting is filed in order for both to be reviewed together.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The second amended account was filed on 6/6/23 (but not e-filed until 6/12/23). Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (2) The 6/10/21 order authorizing the substituted judgment trust authorized the conservator to transfer to herself, as trustee, specific assets listed on exhibit C attached to that order. Those were not the assets that funded this trust. (3) The assets on hand at the end of the conservator's third account, as amended in a "declaration" filed on 6/7/22, are not the assets listed on the trustee's receipt filed on 6/2/23 in the conservatorship case. (4) It is also unclear how the values shown for the assets listed on the receipt filed on 6/2/23 were determined. A third amended first account will likely need to be filed unless the conservator's fourth account clearly confirms the assets that funded this trust on 12/1/21, as reflected on the receipt filed on 6/2/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: An accounting petition does not need to narrate the logistics and proceedings, such as continuances, etc.; that is all on the Court's register of actions. The accounting petition should merely report the fiduciary's actions and provide support and authority for any relief she seeks.)
